---
title: "J.D. Cardenas Cartagena"
comments: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/profile.png
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/dccartagena
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dccartagena
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_ID
    - icon: person-badge-fill
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_ORCID
---

## About Me

I am a researcher and engineer at the intersection of **applied mathematics**, **control theory**, and **machine learning**. My work focuses on designing **trustworthy artificial intelligence** agents for safety-critical mechatronics systems — building techniques that are high-performing, provably safe, and interpretable.

I recently joined [Youwe Concept B.V.](https://youwe.nl) in The Netherlands as a Machine Learning Engineer, and I am finalizing my Ph.D. in Engineering and Science at the University of Agder, Norway. Prior to that, I was a Lecturer in Artificial Intelligence at the University of Groningen, where I received the **Teacher of the Year Award for AI & CCS** in 2025.

My academic background spans three continents:

- **Ph.D.** in Engineering and Science — University of Agder, Norway *(expected 2026)*
- **M.Sc.** in Mathematics and Statistics — Queen's University, Canada
- **B.S.** in Mechatronics Engineering — EIA University, Colombia

## Research Interests

- **Safe Reinforcement Learning** — constrained and risk-aware policy optimization
- **Data-Driven Control** — adaptive and learning-based control for dynamical systems
- **Gaussian Processes** — probabilistic uncertainty quantification in control systems
- **Trustworthy AI** — interpretability, robustness, and formal verification of autonomous systems

## Teaching & Mentoring

At the University of Groningen I taught and co-designed undergraduate and graduate courses in Signals & Systems, Reinforcement Learning, and Machine Learning. I have mentored Bachelor's and Master's thesis students in applying rigorous AI methodologies to real-world engineering problems.

Outside academia I participate in science outreach and education initiatives aimed at engaging young people in research and engineering — particularly through programs in Colombia such as [Clubes de Ciencia Colombia](https://clubesdeciencia.co) and [Parque Explora](https://www.parqueexplora.org).

## Contact

I am open to academic collaborations, speaking invitations, and research discussions. Reach out via [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dccartagena) or [GitHub](https://github.com/dccartagena).
